data.title: Royal Philips, Mass General Collaborate to Enhance Patient Care Through Live AI-Powered Tools
data.description:
Royal Philips and Mass General Brigham said Friday they signed a new partnership to develop and deploy advanced data infrastructure and artificial intelligence to enhance patient care.

Financial terms were not disclosed.

The parties intend to combine their specialties to gather detailed data from medical devices and other sources to form a single, real-time data and insights system, they added.

Mass General will integrate its biomedical engineering team, clinical sub-specialists, digital teams, and internal AI business unit with Philips' research and business teams, the companies said.

data.title: Endeavour Silver Surface Drilling at Bolanitos Intersects High-Grade Mineralization
data.description:
Endeavour Silver on Friday said recent surface drilling intersected high-grade gold and silver mineralization at the Bolanitos Mine's La Luz vein in Mexico.

Highlights include 542 grams per tonne (gpt) silver equivalent over a 1.02 m estimated true width (ETW) in hole LZ46-3; and 1,258 gpt silver equivalent over a 1.62 m ETW, in hole LZ48-1.

The 2024/2025 program focused on drilling the La Luz vein, and the high grade results provides opportunities to replace reserves, increase resources and extend mine life, Endeavour added.

"For more than 17 years, our exploration success has been integral to the ongoing operations at Bolanitos," said Chief Executive Dan Dickson. "The latest results highlight our focus on efficient, cost-effective exploration, which continues to extend the mine's life."

Endeavour Silver was last seen down US$0.07, to US$1.68, in U.S. pre-market trading.

data.title: Update: Walt Disney's ESPN, Major League Baseball to End Media Rights Partnership
data.description:
(Updates with ESPN's statement on X.)

Walt Disney' ESPN confirmed that it has opted out of its partnership with Major League Baseball, or MLB, in a Friday post on social media platform X.

ESPN said in the post that it remains open to exploring other ways to serve the league's fans after 2025.

The Wall Street Journal reported the end of the parties' media rights partnership and said ESPN was due to pay the league $550 million per year from 2026 to 2028 in airing rights.

MLB Commissioner Rob Manfred told team owners that ESPN made an "aggressive effort to reduce rights fees", according to a letter seen by WSJ.

Manfred said that the league had "not been pleased with the minimal coverage that MLB has received on ESPN's platforms," and that it does not think pay TV is the best platform for its content and plans to explore opportunities with a new broadcast or streaming partner.

Manfred also showed interest in selling a national streaming package that would centralize local media rights after MLB's deals with Fox and Turner Sports expire in 2028, according to the report.

MLB did not immediately respond to MT Newswires' request for comment.

data.title: Market Chatter: Foxconn Looks to Partner With Honda, Other Japan Automakers in EV Space
data.description:
Taiwanese electronics manufacturer Foxconn is looking to partner with Honda Motor together with Nissan Motor and Mitsubishi Motors in the electric vehicle space, Nikkei reported Friday, citing a source familiar with the matter.

Foxconn Chairman Young Liu said he is looking to collaborate on the design and manufacture of EVs on a contract basis, although the company does not plan to establish its own automobile brand, the report said.

Foxconn, Honda, Nissan and Mitsubishi did not immediately reply to MT Newswires' request for comment.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: UScellular Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Results
data.description:
United States Cellular Corporation, a leading wireless telecommunications provider, has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2024. The company reported a decrease in total operating revenues and net income compared to the previous year, alongside several strategic initiatives and corporate developments.

Financial Highlights

For the fourth quarter of 2024, UScellular reported total operating revenues of $970 million, a 3% decrease from $1,000 million in the same period of 2023. Net income attributable to UScellular shareholders was $5 million, down from $14 million in the fourth quarter of 2023. Diluted earnings per share (EPS) for the quarter were $0.05, compared to $0.16 in the previous year.

For the full year 2024, total operating revenues were $3,770 million, a 3% decline from $3,906 million in 2023. The company reported a net loss attributable to shareholders of $39 million, or $(0.46) per diluted share, compared to a net income of $54 million, or $0.63 per diluted share, in 2023. Excluding a third-quarter license impairment of $136 million, net income for 2024 was $63 million, or $0.71 per diluted share.

Business and Operational Highlights

UScellular saw improvements in its wireless operating results, with reduced postpaid and prepaid net losses and churn rates. The company also reported a 27% growth in fixed wireless customers. Cash flows from operating activities and free cash flow increased year-over-year, and the ongoing deployment of the 5G mid-band network provided additional capacity and faster speeds for customers.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

In 2024, UScellular announced several transactions as part of its strategic alternatives review. These included a Securities Purchase Agreement with T-Mobile to sell its wireless operations and select spectrum assets, expected to close in mid-2025. Additionally, the company entered into License Purchase Agreements with Verizon, Nsight Spectrum, Nex-Tech Wireless, and AT&T for the sale of various spectrum licenses.

During 2024, UScellular repurchased 939,999 of its Common Shares for $55 million.

Management's Perspective

Laurent Therivel, UScellular President and CEO, commented, "In 2024, UScellular made significant progress in enhancing shareholder value, while remaining steadfast in its mission of connecting people to what matters most. We also maintained strong financial discipline resulting in solid growth in profitability and free cash flow. While subscriber results remained negative, we saw meaningful improvement in postpaid and prepaid additions in the third and fourth quarters of 2024. We intend to build on this momentum and will continue to invest in our customers and our network while working towards closing the transactions that we have previously announced."

Future Outlook

Due to the pending transaction with T-Mobile, UScellular is not providing financial guidance for 2025.

SEC Filing: UNITED STATES CELLULAR CORP [ USM ] - 8-K - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: Telephone and Data Systems, Inc. Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Results
data.description:
Telephone and Data Systems, Inc. (TDS) has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2024. The company, which provides wireless, broadband, video, and voice services through its subsidiaries UScellular and TDS Telecom, reported a decrease in total operating revenues and net income compared to the previous year.

Financial Highlights

For the fourth quarter of 2024, TDS reported total operating revenues of $1,240 million, a 6% decrease from $1,313 million in the same period of 2023. The net loss attributable to TDS common shareholders was $(11) million, or $(0.10) per diluted share, compared to a net loss of $(523) million, or $(4.64) per diluted share, in the fourth quarter of 2023. For the full year 2024, total operating revenues were $4,964 million, down 4% from $5,160 million in 2023. The net loss attributable to TDS common shareholders for the year was $(97) million, or $(0.85) per diluted share, compared to $(569) million, or $(5.06) per diluted share, in 2023.

Business and Operational Highlights

UScellular, a subsidiary of TDS, announced multiple transactions related to its strategic alternatives review, including a significant transaction with T-Mobile and four spectrum transactions with various mobile network operators. The company also reported improvements in wireless operating results, with postpaid and prepaid net losses and churn rates showing positive trends. Fixed wireless customers grew by 27%, and cash flows from operating activities and free cash flow increased year over year. UScellular continued its 5G mid-band network deployment, enhancing capacity and speed for customers.

TDS Telecom exceeded its full-year 2024 fiber address goal by delivering 129,000 marketable fiber service addresses. The company expanded its footprint by 6%, increasing total service addresses to 1.8 million. Residential broadband connections grew by 2%, and residential revenue per connection increased by 5%. Total wireline expansion residential revenues grew to $114 million, up from $75 million in 2023.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

In 2024, TDS and UScellular entered into a Securities Purchase Agreement to sell UScellular's wireless operations and select spectrum assets to T-Mobile, with the transaction expected to close in mid-2025. Additionally, UScellular entered into agreements with Verizon Communications, Inc., Nsight Spectrum, LLC, Nex-Tech Wireless, LLC, and New Cingular Wireless PCS, LLC (AT&T) for the sale of various spectrum licenses. Due to the pending transaction with T-Mobile, UScellular is not providing 2025 financial guidance.

Management's Perspective

Walter C. D. Carlson, TDS President and CEO, commented on the company's progress in 2024, highlighting the strategic review of alternatives at UScellular and investments in high-quality networks. Carlson emphasized the ongoing fiber expansion at TDS Telecom and the focus on operational priorities and growing the tower business at UScellular.

Future Outlook

TDS Telecom provided guidance for 2025, estimating total operating revenues between $1,030 million and $1,070 million, adjusted OIBDA between $310 million and $350 million, and adjusted EBITDA between $320 million and $360 million. Capital expenditures are expected to range from $375 million to $425 million. The company plans to continue its fiber expansion and participate in the FCC’s Enhanced Alternative Connect America Cost Model program, aiming to bring a fiber-rich network to over 150,000 new marketable service addresses throughout the country.

SEC Filing: TELEPHONE & DATA SYSTEMS INC /DE/ [ TDS ] - 8-K - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: Benitec Biopharma Price Target Maintained With a $28.00/Share by HC Wainwright & Co.
Astronics Ordered to Pay Lufthansa Technik $11.9 Million in Patent Infringement Case
data.description:
Astronics said Friday it has been ordered by a UK court to pay $11.9 million to Lufthansa Technik to settle a patent infringement dispute.

The company said any additional amounts related to matters peripheral to the case will be determined at a hearing scheduled for next month.

Astronics said it expects payment of the final liability to be required in Q2, and that any appeal would likely be heard in early 2026.

Astronics' shares were down 2% in premarket trading.

data.title: Mexico GDP Contraction Confirmed at 3-Year High
data.description:
The Mexican gross domestic product contracted by 0.6% from the previous quarter in the final three months of 2024, trimming the 1.1% expansion in the previous period and in line with preliminary estimates to confirm the sharp miss of expectations of a 0.2% drop.

It was the sharpest contraction since the third quarter of 2021, aligned with signals from the central bank that larger rate cuts may be appropriate this year, especially if the US government follows through with tariff threats.

Output from primary activities plunged by 8.5% from the previous quarter, while output from industry fell by 1.5%.

In turn, services output edged higher by 0.2%.

On an annual basis, the Mexican GDP expanded by 0.5% in the fourth quarter, revised lower from the preliminary reading of 0.6%.

For the full year, the Mexican economy expanded by 1.2%.

data.title: Imperial Provides Red Chris Mine 2024 Production Update and 2025 Outlook
data.description:
Imperial Metals overnight Thursday reported 2024 metal production from the Red Chris mine of 85.3 million pounds of copper and 59,811 ounces of gold, up from 57.1 million pounds of copper and 46,046 ounces of gold in 2023, on higher grades and recoveries for both metals.

The company said its 30% share of Red Chris mine production for 2024 was 25.5 million pounds of copper up from 17.1 million pounds copper in 2023 and 17,943 ounces of gold up from 13,814 ounces gold in 2023.

Newmont is forecasting the Red Chris mine will produce 88 million pounds of copper and 86,000 ounces of gold in 2025.

The company said 2025 production target for Mount Polley is 25.0 - 27.0 million pounds copper and 35,000 - 40,000 ounces gold. Phase 4 Springer Pit ore, which has a higher recoverable copper grade is targeted to be fully mined by Q3 of 2025, with the lower copper grade from Phase 5 pushback in the Springer pit delivering process ore in the Q4.

Shares of the company closed down $0.02 at $2.440 on Thursday on the Toronto Stock Exchange.

data.title: Mexico's Economic Output Shrinks in Fourth Quarter
data.description:
By Anthony Harrup

MEXICO CITY--Mexico's economy contracted in the fourth quarter on lower industrial output and agricultural production, the first quarterly decline in more than three years.

Gross domestic product, a measure of output in goods and services, fell 0.6% in seasonally adjusted terms from the third quarter, statistics institute Inegi said Friday. The decline was in line with the preliminary reading at the end of January. Industrial output fell 1.5% and agricultural production was down 8.5%, while services increased 0.2%.

GDP was up 0.5% unadjusted from the year-earlier quarter, bringing growth for all of 2024 to 1.5%, compared with 3.3% in 2023.

The Bank of Mexico this week cut its growth estimate for 2025 to 0.6% from 1.2%, citing a weaker-than-expected finish to 2024 and the impact that uncertainty over U.S. trade policy toward Mexico could have on private consumption and to a greater extent on investment.

Household consumption is likely to remain resilient this year, supported by remittances, credit and wage growth and continued government transfers, said Carlos Capistran, economist for Latin America and Canada at Bank of America Securities.

"We believe consumption will get out of the soft patch it is currently in and will outpace GDP growth in 2025," he said in a note.

Write to Anthony Harrup at anthony.harrup@wsj.com

data.title: CARE Affirms AA- Rating on Surya Roshni's Long-term Bank Financing; Outlook Stable
data.description:
CARE Ratings retained its AA- rating on lighting specialist Surya Roshni's long-term bank facilities and A1+ rating on the company's short-term bank financing.

The rating outlook is stable, according to a Friday filing with the Indian exchanges.

data.title: Canagold Provides Antimony Resource Estimate for New Polaris Gold Project in British Columbia
data.description:
Canagold Resources reported Friday an updated resource estimate for antimony at the New Polaris gold project in British Columbia.

The study outlined 5,630 tonnes of antimony metal within the base case indicated gold resource, and 1,195 tonnes of antimony metal within the base case inferred gold resource.

"Past metallurgical testing has shown excellent antimony recovery rates within the New Polaris concentrate, highlighting its potential to enhance future revenue streams," CEO Catalin Kilofliski said.

For 2025, Canagold said it will conduct further analysis of the antimony resource and its expansion potential, along with additional metallurgical testing, to determine the best processing methods for producing a commercially viable antimony.

"With New Polaris at an advanced stage of development, Canagold is strategically positioned as a leading Canadian company ready to play a key role in strengthening Canada's critical mineral supply," Kilofliski said.

data.title: Holcim to Name North American Business Amrize After Planned Spinoff
data.description:
By Pierre Bertrand

Holcim said that its business in North America will be renamed to Amrize after its planned spinoff.

The new name and branding will go into effect after the spinoff, expected by the end of the first half. Until then, the business will operate as part of Holcim, the Swiss-based supplier of building materials said Friday.

Holcim added it intends to list Amrize shares on the New York Stock Exchange with an additional listing in Switzerland.

Holcim said in late January 2024 that it intended to separate out its North American operations into a business that could be worth more than $30 billion.

Write to Pierre Bertrand at pierre.bertrand@wsj.com

data.title: Earnings Flash (TDS) Telephone And Data Systems, Posts Q4 Adjusted Loss $-0.09 Per Share, vs. FactSet Est of $-0.38
AlphaValue/Baader Europe Warns of Potentially Turbulent FY25 for Airbus
data.description:
AlphaValue/Baader Europe maintains its positive stance on Airbus as a key player in the aerospace industry after solid 2024 results but highlighted challenges that could make 2025 a turbulent year.

"Airbus has certainly delivered, posting solid numbers that met expectations on both the top-line and profitability side, while FCF blew past its guidance. While the [1 euro] special dividend was a pleasant surprise, supply chain headaches, particularly with narrow-body engines, remain a persistent issue," analysts said in a Thursday note. "The numbers look fine, but don't expect smooth skies ahead-turbulent times may be just around the corner."

The Paris-listed aircraft maker delivered a 6% year-over-year rise in 2024 revenue to 69.2 billion euros and an adjusted EBIT of 5.4 billion euros, broadly in line with consensus. On the other hand, Airbus' guidance for 2025 was deemed by analysts as "less impressive," as it projects 820 aircraft deliveries and adjusted EBIT of 7 billion euros, both below the consensus of 838 deliveries and 7.3 billion euros.

The research firm rates the stock at add, with a target price of 190 euros.

data.title: Earnings Flash (USM) United States Cellular Posts Q4 Adjusted EPS $0.05, vs. FactSet Est of $-0.11
UBS Comments on Canadian Dollar
data.description:
Following the United States administration's pause on applying tariffs to Canadian goods at the beginning of February, USDCAD came off its highs and now trades 2.5% lower — mostly driven by the broader US dollar (USD) strength rather than CAD-related idiosyncratic factors, said UBS.

The bank thinks financial markets have become complacent about tariff risks, and rate differentials suggest the pair should trade around 1.44.

As a consequence, UBS sees value in selling downside below 1.4030 over the coming month, before the looming April tariff deadline.

data.title: Earnings Flash (USM) United States Cellular Posts Q4 Revenue $970M, vs. FactSet Est of $960.3M
The stock market is expensive. But there's still one bull case not priced in, says Bank of America.
data.description:
By Jamie Chisholm

Bank of America says heavily regulated sectors have yet to fully rally

Stocks are hanging in there. After Thursday's Walmart wobble, broader losses were pared as investors once again bought the dip. The S&P 500 still sits just 0.4% shy of its record high.

The market's stoicism comes even as Bank of America says 19 out of 20 valuation metrics it tracks show the S&P 500 SPX "is trading at more expensive levels than historical averages."

However, the BofA team, led by equity and quant strategist Savita Subramanian, observes the Wall Street equity barometer has been statistically expensive for a while, and the premium may be warranted for a number of reasons.

For example, the index in recent years has had an increasing weighting of very profitable technology stocks, while earnings visibility has improved alongside healthier balance sheets. Corporations have successfully shifted from the era of very low cost capital to embrace greater efficiency.

And amid all the optimism, BofA says there is one bullish factor that is still yet to be priced in: deregulation.

Recent economic policy headlines out of Washington "have been dominated by tariffs - perceived to be growth-negative - but a growth-positive component of Trump 2.0 is deregulation, via cost cutting and efficiency gains," says BofA.

The industries that should have the most to gain from the cutting of red tape are those that are most tightly wrapped up in it.

But BofA notes that these groups, such as consumer sectors, large banks, commodities sectors, transports and capital goods, are trading at steep discounts to regulation-light groups, like technology, media, telecoms and the consumer & professional services sector.

"We find a strong inverse relationship between the number of regulations...and forward P/E ratios - i.e., more expensive = less regulated," says BofA.

Among the starkest contrasts in this regulation and valuation correlation is that of technology and financials, which have seen opposite fates from 2008, says BofA.

Since then tech enjoyed growth, access to capital and the lightest increase in regulations relative to all other S&P 500 sectors. Financials, on the other hand, saw the biggest increase in regulations and spent the next decade repairing balance sheets and consolidating capacity, according to BofA.

"Today the two sit at opposite ends of the valuation spectrum, but history suggests that deregulation could ignite equal and opposite reactions - a relative re-rating in financials vs a relative de-rating in tech," says BofA.

Markets

U.S. stock-index futures (ES00) (YM00) (NQ00) are higher as benchmark Treasury yields dip. The dollar index DXY is higher, while oil prices (CL.1) slip and gold (GC00) is trading around $2,930 an ounce.

   Key asset performance                                                Last       5d     1m      YTD     1y 
   S&P 500                                                              6117.52    0.04%  -0.02%  4.01%   20.26% 
   Nasdaq Composite                                                     19,962.36  0.08%  -0.46%  3.37%   24.44% 
   10-year Treasury                                                     4.493      1.30   -4.80   -8.30   21.05 
   Gold                                                                 2944.7     1.76%  6.03%   11.57%  43.94% 
   Oil                                                                  71.88      1.86%  -3.65%  0.01%   -6.13% 
   Data: MarketWatch. Treasury yields change expressed in basis points

The buzz

U.S. economic data due on Friday include the S&P flash services and manufacturing PMIs, due at 9:45 a.m. Eastern. The final reading of February consumer sentiment will be released at 10:00 a.m., alongside existing home sales for January.

Some $2.7 trillion worth of option contracts are set to expire on Friday, according to Goldman Sachs.

Shares of Nissan (JP:7201) jumped nearly 10% after the Financial Times reported "a high-level Japanese group" were drawing up plans to ask Elon Musk's Tesla (TSLA) to invest in the struggling car maker. How the companies could fit together.

Celsius Holdings (CELH) shares are topping the leaderboard in premarket action, leaping 35% after the drink maker posted better-than-expected earnings and announced the $1.8 billion purchase of rival Alani Nu.

Block shares (XYZ) skidded 7% as the payments company's results were not well received by investors.

Fed Vice Chairman Philip Jefferson is due to speak at 11:30 a.m. and San Francisco Fed President Mary Daly makes opening remarks at the same time.

Best of the web

The cryptocurrency scam that turned a small town against itself.

Trump isn't the first president to slash the federal workforce. How he's doing it is a different story.

The wild economics behind Ferrari's domination of the luxury car market.

The chart

There's been a lot of talk of late about the recent outperformance of markets like Germany in relation to the U.S. The chart below from Nicholas Colas, co-founder of DataTrek Research, which depicts the correlation between the S&P 500 and the MSCI EAFE index (non-US developed economies like Europe and Japan), shows just how extreme things have gotten.

"We've seen this sort of anomalous price action in numerous other asset classes and even single stocks over the years, and it always ends with a short-term correction in asset prices that force correlations back to their long run average. In the case of EAFE versus U.S. large caps, we believe that non-U.S. stocks are more likely to weaken from here just given their recent outperformance," says Colas.

-Jamie Chisholm
